title: Dataset of Concurrent EEG, ECG, and Behavior with Multiple Doses of transcranial Electrical Stimulation - BIDS

affiliations.name: The City College of New York

description: A comprehensive dataset combining high-density electroencephalography (EEG) with concurrent electrocardiography (ECG), electrooculography (EOG), and continuous behavioral metrics during transcranial electrical stimulation (tES). The dataset encompasses 19 participants across 62 sessions performing a vigilance task, with systematic application of nine HD-tES montages targeting three cortical regions (frontal, motor, parietal) using three waveforms (DC, 5 Hz, 30 Hz), yielding over 783 stimulation trials. This resource enables investigation of tES dose-response relationships and interactions between stimulation parameters, brain state, physiology, and cognitive performance.
methods_description: EEG, ECG, and EOG were recorded concurrently over 70-minute sessions while participants performed a continuous tracking task (CTT) maintaining a ball at screen center. Participants received periodic 30-second stimulation blocks with nine different HD-tES montage combinations. Experiment 1 included three 70-minute sessions with all 9 stimulation types (3 per session, 4 trials each). Experiment 2 included two 70.5-minute sessions with 2 stimulation types and 20 trials per type. Demographic and self-reported wellness questionnaires were collected before and after each session.
